question.text: Which of the following best describes the role of an annuity in the bucket strategy?

answer.text: It should be the only investment in all three buckets
A small immediate annuity can be included in Bucket 1 to guarantee essential income for the initial 5 years
Annuity should make up 100% of Bucket 3
Annuity is not recommended in the bucket strategy

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In the bucket strategy, a small single-premium immediate annuity can be included in Bucket 1 to provide guaranteed income for the initial retirement years, complementing FDs and SCSS. **The Trap:** Annuity should not dominate any bucket (Option A) or all of Bucket 3 (Option C). Its role is targeted and limited to essential income guarantee. **Cross-Exam Context:** The integration of annuity into the bucket strategy is discussed in Series XVII. It combines the guaranteed income of annuity with the flexibility of other products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Size the annuity to cover the gap between pension (if any) and essential expenses. Don't over-annuitize — the low returns and capital lock-in of annuities make them unsuitable for the full corpus.
